title: A German Shepherd Drifted Out to Sea in a Kayak
script: Dog at sea. Kayak? Off the coast of Northumberland, England, tour skipper Jimmy Reid was running a boat tour to Inner Farne island when the Coastguard radioed: a German shepherd was out at sea — alone — in an inflatable kayak. The wind had blown it offshore with the dog inside. Jimmy's crew spotted it bobbing in the channel. They pulled alongside. The dog panicked and slipped into the water. Crewman Aaron Fordy lunged over the rail and grabbed the dog by the scruff. Aaron started going overboard himself — Jimmy grabbed his leg. Together they hauled the dog back aboard. Soaked but fine. The owner was on shore, watching their kayak drift away, apparently unaware their dog had become a nautical emergency. So who brings a German shepherd on an inflatable kayak to begin with?
